item.title: Hegseth says U.S. will "negotiate with bombs" if needed, U.S. announces more strikes
item.description: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire is appearing to crumble as U.S. forces struck Iran again Wednesday after President Trump pledged Tehran will "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.

item.title: Inflation topped 4% in May as CPI surged to highest level in 3 years
item.description: The Consumer Price Index rose last month at a 4.2% annual rate amid a spike in U.S. energy prices.

item.title: U.S. inflation hits 3-year-high
item.description: The U.S. saw its highest inflation since April 2023 in May, according to the latest numbers from the Bureau of Labor Statistics. Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: U.S. launches more strikes on Iranian targets after Trump promises to hit Iran "hard"
item.description: The U.S. military launched an additional round of strikes on targets within Iran early Thursday morning local time, hours after President Trump vowed to hit Iran "hard."

item.title: Trump says it's time for Iran to "pay the price" as U.S. announces new strikes
item.description: The U.S. military said it launched new attacks on Iran on Wednesday, with President Trump saying it's time for the regime to "pay the price." Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: NBA Finals Game 4 watch party outside Madison Square Garden canceled as heightened security remains
item.description: The New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs will square off in Game 4 of the NBA Finals Wednesday night, but fans hoping to watch the game outside Madison Square Garden will have to find new plans. CBS News reporter Jared Ochacher has more.

item.title: Bill Gates says his Epstein ties were a "grave error in judgement" on Capitol Hill
item.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates told members of Congress that his ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein were a "grave error in judgment." He was the latest witness to appear before the House Oversight Committee, which is investigating the government's handling of the Epstein case. CBS News' Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: Meta launches program to train workers for data center jobs
item.description: Meta pledged to invest $115 million to train electricians, plumbers and other workers needed to operate data centers.

item.title: What May's inflation report signals about state of U.S. economy
item.description: Inflation in the U.S. accelerated in May to its highest level in more than three years. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady breaks down the numbers.

item.title: Meta offering free training program to fast-track trade jobs
item.description: Meta has created a free five-week training course called America's Workforce Academy to address shortages of jobs like fiber technicians, welders, plumbers and electricians. The workers would help build data centers and then be guaranteed a job in a skilled trade. Marc Morial, president of the National Urban League, joins to discuss his organization's partnership with Meta on the project.

item.title: Why consumer prices jumped in May
item.description: U.S. consumers paid higher prices once again in May, with inflation at its highest level in over three years. According to the latest report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics, consumer prices accelerated at an annual rate of 4.2%. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: U.S. launches new strikes against Iran
item.description: The U.S. military launched a new round of strikes against Iran on Wednesday. It's the second straight day of what CENTCOM calls defensive strikes against Iranian targets. CBS News' Eleanor Watson, Natalie Brand and Sam Vinograd have more.

item.title: Bill Gates tells Congress that meeting Epstein was "a grave error in judgment"
item.description: Bill Gates told members of Congress on Wednesday that Jeffrey Epstein put his philanthropic work at risk, and that meeting him represented "a grave error in judgment."

item.title: House member describes Bill Gates' Epstein testimony
item.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates testified before the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday as part of its investigation into the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and his relationships. Democratic Rep. Stephen Lynch of Massachusetts, who was present at the closed-door testimony, joins to discuss.

item.title: Two women who dated Graham Platner criticize responses to their accusations
item.description: Two of Graham Platner's ex-girlfriends spoke with the Free Press about the reactions to their claims about the Maine Democratic Senate candidate. The Free Press' Audrey Fahlberg joins "The Takeout" to discuss her reporting. The Free Press is a Paramount publication.

item.title: Platner calls Collins "spineless and corrupt" in primary victory speech
item.description: Graham Plater prevailed in Tuesday night's Maine Senate Democratic primary despite more than a week of controversies. He will now face off against Republican Sen. Susan Collins in a race that could determine control of Congress' upper chamber. CBS News political director Fin Gómez joins with analysis.

item.title: The benefits and concerns about smart glasses
item.description: Major tech players are racing to put AI on your face, literally, with smart glasses. Ziad Asghar, senior vice president and general manager of XR, Wearables and Personal AI for Qualcomm, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: U.S. denies entry to Somali World Cup referee due to "security threat"
item.description: Somali World Cup referee Omar Artan is back home after the U.S. denied him entry citing "vetting concerns." CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: U.S. forces conduct more strikes against Iran
item.description: The U.S. launched another round of strikes against Iran in response to its "continued aggression," CENTCOM said on Wednesday evening. CBS News' Eleanor Watson and Natalie Brand have the latest.

item.title: Trump says "I love the inflation" because U.S. is "taking out" Iranian oil
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. has taken out "millions" of barrels of Iranian oil in the dead of night, and said inflation will come down when the war ends.

item.title: American's birth rate has plunged. Are smartphones to blame?
item.description: The iPhone was introduced in 2007, the same year the U.S. birth rate started to slide. The issues could be linked, a new analysis finds.

item.title: How to buy SpaceX shares as its blockbuster IPO readies for liftoff
item.description: SpaceX is setting aside a large chunk of shares for ordinary investors as it seeks to raise a record $75 billion. Here's what to know.

item.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
item.description: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

item.title: Vance says Netanyahu has "certainly gotten some things wrong"
item.description: The vice president said President Trump "has been very clear about what is in our best interest" as the relationship with Israel is tested over the Iran war.

item.title: NASA chief defends choice of all-male crew for Artemis III mission
item.description: NASA's Jared Isaacman says the crew was selected solely based on their experience, expertise and availability for flight assignment.

item.title: Just 1 in 10 Europeans now view the U.S. as an ally, according to poll
item.description: A growing number of Europeans see the U.S. as a rival or an adversary, especially in Denmark, France, Spain and Switzerland, according to the poll.

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Mexico warns Americans of safety risks ahead of World Cup
item.description: The U.S. Embassy said Americans traveling to Mexico for the World Cup should look at advisories for the region they will be visiting.

item.title: Honda recalls 880,000 vehicles over possible rear suspension issue
item.description: The recall affects certain Honda Pilot, Ridgeline, Passport, and Acura MDX vehicles sold in 23 states and the District of Columbia.

item.title: Oversight chair working to have Blanche testify in Epstein probe
item.description: House Oversight and Reform Committee Chairman James Comer said he wants Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to appear before lawmakers in July.

item.title: GOP Rep. Valadao to face progressive Villegas in key California House district
item.description: Progressive Randy Villegas' win is an embarrassing defeat for establishment Democrats amid an intraparty feud about the party's future.
